name: Ourense – circular via San Tomé, Cachamuíña reservoir and Ceboliño
name_original: Ourense – circular por el San Tomé, embalse de Cachamuíña y Ceboliño
region_intro: Hiking around the Mio region in Sur is characterized by its riverine landscapes and historical sites. The area features trails along the Miño River, offering views of its waters and surrounding natural environments. Hikers can explore a mix of woodland paths, thermal areas, and routes leading to waterfalls and ancient settlements. The terrain generally includes moderate elevation changes, making it suitable for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around the Mio The most popular hiking route is Miño River Thermal Trail, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This moderate path follows the Miño River, known for its thermal features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Augacaída Waterfall and Castro de Marce – circular from Marce, an easy 3.1 miles (4.9 km) path. This route leads to a waterfall and an ancient fort, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical interest. Local hikers also love the Camiño do Miño Trail, a 8.2 miles (13.2 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around the Mio is defined by river paths, thermal springs, and historical sites, including ancient Roman walls and forts. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate trails. The routes in Mio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. More than 2,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Mio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Mio region?
A: The Mio region offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 140 trails recorded on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more challenging routes, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners in Mio?
A: Yes, Mio has numerous easy trails perfect for families and beginners. Approximately 68 routes are classified as easy. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979353635/augacaida-waterfall-and-castro-de-marce-circular-from-marce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall and Castro de Marce – circular from Marce&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.1-mile path combining natural beauty with historical interest. The region's trails often feature moderate elevation changes, making them accessible.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Mio?
A: Hiking in Mio offers diverse natural landscapes, primarily characterized by riverine environments along the Miño River. You'll encounter lush woodlands, thermal areas, and trails leading to waterfalls. The region is known for its semi-primitive experience, with opportunities to observe various plant life and wildlife.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls in the Mio area?
A: Yes, the Mio region is home to beautiful waterfalls. A notable route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979353635/augacaida-waterfall-and-castro-de-marce-circular-from-marce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall and Castro de Marce – circular from Marce&lt;/a&gt;, which directly leads to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5016863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Mio?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Mio are designed as circular routes, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979353635/augacaida-waterfall-and-castro-de-marce-circular-from-marce" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall and Castro de Marce – circular from Marce&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976234765/camino-natural-del-rio-barbantino-circular-from-a-forxa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Natural del Río Barbantiño – circular from A Forxa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore on hikes around Mio?
A: Mio's hiking trails often lead to fascinating historical sites. You can explore ancient settlements like Castro de Marce, or discover Roman-era structures. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e975342627/roman-wall-of-lugo-circular-via-the-ponte-vella-and-the-porta-de-san-pedro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman wall of Lugo – circular via the Ponte Vella and the Porta de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; offers a glimpse into the region's Roman past. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456404" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;As Burgas Roman Baths&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any thermal springs or hot springs accessible via hiking trails in Mio?
A: Yes, the Mio region is known for its thermal features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979336346/mino-river-thermal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño River Thermal Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 6.9-mile route specifically known for following the Miño River and its thermal areas. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3108574" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Outariz Hot Springs&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Mio?
A: The trails in Mio are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 300 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the scenic river paths, the blend of natural beauty with historical sites, and the well-maintained routes that offer a peaceful escape into nature.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Mio?
A: While many trails in Mio are moderate, there are options for more experienced hikers. The region features 7 routes classified as difficult. These trails often involve longer distances and more significant elevation changes, providing a good challenge.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the Miño River?
A: The Miño River is a central feature of the region's hiking appeal. Many trails, including the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979336346/mino-river-thermal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miño River Thermal Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980801668/camino-do-mino-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camiño do Miño Trail&lt;/a&gt;, run alongside or offer sweeping views of its waters and the surrounding river valley, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6496544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra River Valley&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife viewing or birding while hiking in Mio?
A: Yes, Mio's diverse ecosystems, including woodlands and wetlands, provide excellent opportunities for wildlife viewing and birding. Hikers can observe a variety of plants and animals, especially along riverine paths and through forested areas.
tips: Excellent point where to appreciate the Roman bridge of Ourense. The historic stone footbridge that crosses the Miño River was built in the 1st century AD. C. Due to its age, the bridge has been reformed on different occasions. The last one during the 17th century, directed by Melchor de Velasco Agüero, who gave it its current appearance of medieval style.
Reservoir on the da Lonia river, 10 minutes from the city of Ourense. The area has a large green area where you can enjoy great tranquility, as well as a children's playground and a bio-healthy park.

The Cathedral of Ourense, dedicated to Saint Martin, is the main religious monument of the city. Built between the 12th and 13th centuries, this temple has had the honors of a minor basilica since 1867, according to the Pontifical Brief of Pope Pius IX signed on June 30 of that year.
Center and heart of monumental Ourense, its sloping floor is striking, a real rarity among the main squares in Spain.

waypoints.name: View of the Roman Bridge of Ourense
Cathedral of San Martín de Ourense
